title: ELIE - Entomological Label Information Extraction
author: Anonymous
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description:
Natural history museums curate billions of insect specimens, representing a vast and largely untapped source of biodiversity data. While digitization initiatives have significantly increased the availability of high-resolution images of insect specimens and their labels, extracting structured metadata from these labels remains a major bottleneck due to the reliance on manual transcription.  
     
  To address this challenge, we introduce ELIE (Entomological Label Information Extraction)  ,   a semi-automated pipeline powered by computer vision and machine learning techniques to streamline the digitization of insect label metadata. ELIE combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for label detection and classification (printed vs. handwritten), optical character recognition (OCR) using Tesseract and Google Vision, and clustering algorithms for grouping similar label content, with optional human validation for quality assurance.  
     
  The ELIE pipeline has been developed and benchmarked using a diverse suite of annotated datasets comprising 4  3  ,0  69   digitized specimen labels.   These datasets span multiple insect orders, geographic regions, and time periods, and include:  
  •     AntWeb: Annotated labels from global Formicidae collections  
  •     Bees & Bytes: Apoidea labels from the MfN Berlin  
  •     LEPPHIL: Lepidoptera labels from the Philippines  
  •     MCZ_ENT_Boston: Hexapoda labels from the Museum of Comparative Zoology, Harvard  
  •     MfN_LEP_SEASIA: Southeast Asian Pyraloidea labels  
  •     Picturae_MfN: Digitized Hexapoda labels from the MfN Berlin  
  •     USNM_COL_CAM: Beetle labels from South and Central America digitized by the USNM  
     
  Together, these datasets enable robust training, validation, and testing of the ELIE pipeline across varying label formats, image resolutions, and taxonomic groups. ELIE achieves extraction success rates of up to 91.6% on printed labels, significantly reducing the manual workload in digitization workflows. By automating key steps in entomological data extraction, ELIE enhances data accessibility, accelerates biodiversity informatics, and supports large-scale ecological and conservation research.
